title: rbac — published macros
description: Two Blueprint macros for the migration DSL. Both are registered by RbacServiceProvider::boot() during the framework boot phase so every downstream migration can call them regardless of module priority.
namespace_note: Blueprint macros extend `Illuminate\Database\Schema\Blueprint`. Registered on the framework blueprint class — no rbac dependency is added to consumers because Blueprint is a Laravel core class.

macros.name: Blueprint::guardable_columns

columns.name: guard_name
columns.description: Enum-like column (validated at write time by the observer) with values `sanctum` OR `platform_admin`. Mirrors spatie's own `guard_name` on roles + permissions.

macros.description: Adds the `guard_name` column + index used by every table participating in the guard-scoping contract. Spatie's own roles + permissions tables already ship this column — the macro is for cross-module extensions (e.g. a future `role_assignment_requests` table in access/requests) that need to carry the guard.

macros.name: Blueprint::rbac_scoped_columns

columns.name: application_id
columns.description: FK to applications.id, ON DELETE RESTRICT. NULL = platform-plane row.

columns.name: tenant_id
columns.description: FK to tenants.id, ON DELETE CASCADE (tenant deletion cascades to their RBAC rows). NULL = system row (shipped by Stackra).

indexes.description: Composite index for the resolver's hot-path lookups on `(application_id, tenant_id)`.

macros.description: Adds the two-column scope contract carried by every RBAC table (both the roles / permissions tables AND their pivots for denormalisation). Idempotent — safe to call in either `create` or `table` migrations.
macros.boot_order_note: Registered by `RbacServiceProvider::boot()`. Because Laravel loads every ServiceProvider's `boot()` before running any migration, the macro is available to every migration regardless of the rbac module's priority (30).

note: The macros are DSL sugar — the same columns could be written by hand. The value is centralising the shape so a future change (e.g. tightening the FK constraint) lands in one place.
